audienceSections.normal: In Sami’s morning routine, you will use [[grammar:يَفْعَلُ + subject]]. A present verb can come before the person doing the action. In يَشْرَبُ سَامِي مَاءً, [[grammar:يَشْرَبُ سَامِي]] means ‘Sami drinks.’ Keep the verb matched to the subject; do not treat سَامِي as the object.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:يَفْعَلُ + subject]] in Sami’s morning routine. The chapter supplies elementary VSO clauses with third-person masculine singular imperfect verbs. In يَشْرَبُ سَامِي مَاءً, سَامِي is the nominative postverbal subject and مَاءً the accusative object.

audienceSections.normal: A present verb can come before the person doing the action. In يَشْرَبُ سَامِي مَاءً, [[grammar:يَشْرَبُ سَامِي]] means ‘Sami drinks.’ Keep the verb matched to the subject; do not treat سَامِي as the object.
audienceSections.expert: The chapter supplies elementary VSO clauses with third-person masculine singular imperfect verbs. In يَشْرَبُ سَامِي مَاءً, سَامِي is the nominative postverbal subject and مَاءً the accusative object.

audienceSections.normal: The Arabic lines are in Modern Standard Arabic. The vowel marks show short vowels and case endings, so read every marked vowel instead of guessing from the bare consonants.
audienceSections.expert: The reading uses fully vocalized Modern Standard Arabic. Lexical citation forms follow the established perfect-stem convention for verbs, while the learner text preserves the attested inflected form and case vocalization.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact arabic line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:يَفْعَلُ + subject]] where it appears.

- يَسْتَيْقِظُ سَامِي صَبَاحًا. — Sami wakes up in the morning.
- يَشْرَبُ سَامِي مَاءً. — Sami drinks water.
- يَأْكُلُ سَامِي خُبْزًا. — Sami eats bread.
- يَقْرَأُ سَامِي كِتَابًا. — Sami reads a book.
- ثُمَّ يَأْخُذُ الْقَلَمَ. — Then he takes the pen.
- وَيَذْهَبُ إِلَى الْمَكْتَبِ. — And he goes to the desk.
breakdown.expert:
These exact reading lines provide the distributional evidence for [[grammar:يَفْعَلُ + subject]]. The grammar section above analyzes the relevant morphology and syntax.

- يَقْرَأُ سَامِي كِتَابًا. — Sami reads a book.
- ثُمَّ يَأْخُذُ الْقَلَمَ. — Then he takes the pen.
